creator: tilman.blumhagen@googlemail.com
text:
When starting xscreensaver all CPU cores are under high load and I hear my hard disk suddenly being very active. The file /var/log/Xorg.0.log grows unusually fast (aroung 1KiB per second).

Watching the logfile growing:
$ ls -lh /var/log/Xorg.0.log
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel   997k Nov 10 12:03 /var/log/Xorg.0.log
$ ls -lh /var/log/Xorg.0.log
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel   1.1M Nov 10 12:03 /var/log/Xorg.0.log

Lines in the logfile (it seems to be repeatedly the same stuff):
[ 10341.249] (II) intel(0): Modeline "1024x768"x0.0   78.75  1024 1040 1136 1312  768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync (60.0 kHz)
[ 10341.249] (II) intel(0): Modeline "1024x768"x0.0   75.00  1024 1048 1184 1328  768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync (56.5 kHz)
[ 10341.249] (II) intel(0): Modeline "1024x768"x0.0   65.00  1024 1048 1184 1344  768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync (48.4 kHz)
[ 10341.249] (II) intel(0): Modeline "832x624"x0.0   57.28  832 864 928 1152  624 625 628 667 -hsync -vsync (49.7 kHz)

)
[ 10341.595] (II) intel(0): Modeline "800x600"x0.0   40.00  800 840 968 1056  600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync (37.9 kHz)

)

How-To-Repeat: run "xscreensaver -nosplash". It also happens while the settings dialog is open. So its probably does not depend on how xscreensaver is started.
